summary: Auth module refactoring verified - All auth routes successfully extracted from server.py to backend/routes/auth.py. Backend: 18/18 tests passed. Frontend: Login flow working correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: Auth module properly separated into backend/routes/auth.py with setup_auth_routes() function
Old auth routes in server.py correctly disabled (decorators commented out)
Auth module correctly imported and initialized with required dependencies (db, get_current_user, send_email, limiter, csrf_tokens, config)
Helper functions (hash_password, verify_password, validate_password_strength, create_access_token) correctly attached to router for external access
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth routes have been successfully refactored into backend/routes/auth.py module. All endpoints working: login, logout, /auth/me, password-rules, forgot-password. The module uses setup_auth_routes() to initialize with db, get_current_user, send_email, limiter, csrf_tokens, and config. Frontend login navigates to /dashboard on success.

features_verified.POST /api/auth/login - valid credentials: PASS - Returns access_token, csrf_token, user data
features_verified.GET /api/auth/me - authenticated: PASS - Returns user data without password_hash
features_verified.POST /api/auth/logout: PASS - Clears session, returns success message
features_verified.GET /api/auth/password-rules: PASS - Returns all password validation rules
features_verified.POST /api/auth/forgot-password: PASS - Returns success for both existing and non-existing emails (prevents enumeration)
features_verified.Frontend Login Flow: PASS - Login page loads, form fills, navigates to /dashboard on success
